judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with equivalent substance; Report 1 is marginally more thorough (e.g., seed data evidence, UI/backend mismatch, fail-open bool coercion) but both fully satisfy the stated expectations.

report_1.evidence: A clinic that has never opened the setting has no `auto_send_reminders` key in its JSONB `tenant.settings` dict, so `tenant.settings.get("auto_send_reminders")` returns `None`, and the resolver returns `True`. The deciding code is the function `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py`, specifically the missing-value branch at lines 11–12

report_2.evidence: A clinic that has never opened the setting WILL have reminders sent automatically.** The deciding code is `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py`, lines 11–12: ... A clinic that never opened the setting has no `auto_send_reminders` key in its JSONB `tenant.settings`, so `.get()` returns `None` and the function returns `True`.

report_1.evidence: I would change `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12`: the `is None` branch should `return False` (equivalently, drop the special case and require the stored value to be exactly `True`).

report_2.evidence: I would change `slotwise/backend/app/feature_flags.py:12` from `return True` to `return False`

report_1.evidence: Note the contrast with `compact_calendar_view_enabled` (lines 4–7 of the same file): default-on is defensible *there* because it's a cosmetic display preference — that reasoning does not transfer to outbound messaging.

report_2.evidence: Default-on is defensible for `compact_calendar_view` — a harmless display preference where you want everyone to get the nicer view. Reusing that pattern for a flag that triggers outbound patient messaging transplants a default across a meaningful category boundary

report_1.evidence: Add an environment-level master switch ANDed with the per-tenant value. Currently the per-tenant flag is the *only* gate on the job — there is no kill switch.

report_2.evidence: Add a global kill switch above the per-tenant flag. `reminders.run()` iterates all tenants with no environment-level or operational gate; if the job misbehaves after launch there is no way to stop sends short of a deploy.
